title: asclepias longifolia subsp. hirtella
intro: Asclepias longifolia subsp. hirtella is a subspecies of the Asclepias longifolia plant, also known as the long-leaved milkweed, which is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other health issues. The health benefits of Asclepias longifolia subsp. hirtella include its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
